DigiKey purchase assumptions and design-critical notes
Prepared 2026-07-21. Change the blue input cells before ordering; stock and prices are not locked.
Confirm XTMA1 versus XTMA2 and the correct provisioned authentication device before purchase.
These parts are not necessarily interchangeable or blank-programmable in your workflow.
PCB footprint is a right-angle male header; schematic wording says socket.
Wrong gender may fit the PCB but not your cable.
Secure U1 first and verify the current DigiKey lifecycle/stock status.
The listing appeared limited / last-time-buy when checked.
Exact MPN had no DigiKey stock; validate any cross-reference for voltage, pinout and gate drive.
A casual BSS138 substitution may not preserve all limits.
Buy the specified 100 V C0G/NP0 parts, not generic 100 nF X7R parts.
Capacitance stability and voltage rating affect the resonant power stage.
The initial JLC match shown in the screenshot was only 50 V X7R.
Sense error and temperature drift affect current control/protection.
Validate MP-A11 geometry, inductance, shielding, DCR and current rating before any substitution.
Inductance alone is not enough for a Qi transmitter coil.
Open every link immediately before checkout and fill Unit Price / Order Status.
Distributor status changes; this workbook is a buying checklist, not a stock reservation.
